seo.pageTitle: Busan Cruise Shore Excursion: Small-Group Day Tour | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: 9-hour shared-van shore excursion from Busan cruise terminal: UN Memorial, Taejongdae cliffs, Gamcheon Culture Village, Yongdusan Park, Jagalchi Market.
seo.title: 부산 크루즈 기항지 소그룹 관광 · 9시간 | AtoC Korea
seo.description: 부산 크루즈 기항지 소그룹 9시간 투어: 유엔기념공원 → 태종대 해안 절벽 → 감천문화마을 → 용두산공원·부산타워 → 자갈치시장. 귀선 시각 보장.

catalog_card.title: 부산 크루즈 기항지 소그룹 관광 (합승 밴, 9시간)
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: 유엔기념공원(세계 유일 유엔 군인 묘지), 태종대(부산 남단 해안 절벽 공원), 감천문화마을(부산의 마추픽추), 용두산공원·부산타워, 자갈치수산시장까지 크루즈 기항지 시간에 맞춘 소그룹 투어입니다. 귀선 시각 60분 전 터미널 귀환 보장.
catalog_card.subtitle: 크루즈 터미널에서 출발해 부산 5대 명소를 9시간 합승 밴으로 돌아봅니다.

headlineLine2: 부산 시그니처 코스를 합승 밴으로 하루에

price.priceNote: Original price USD 65 on the operator's listing. AtoC Korea sale price applies a flat 10% discount.

hero.tagline: 크루즈 일정에 맞춰 조율된 부산 5대 명소 — 다른 크루즈 여행자들과 함께하는 합승 밴 하루 투어

meta.duration: ≈ 9 hours, calibrated to your cruise's port-call window

galleryItems.alt: Colorful houses cascading down a hillside in Gamcheon Culture Village
galleryItems.caption: Gamcheon Culture Village — painted in 2009 as part of Busan's public-art renewal of the postwar refugee neighborhood.
galleryItems.atmosphere: Bright pastels, narrow alleys, hillside stairs

galleryItems.alt: Sea cliffs with a white lighthouse on the horizon
galleryItems.caption: Taejongdae coastal park, southern tip of Yeongdo Island. Tsushima visible on clear winter days.
galleryItems.atmosphere: Open ocean, cliff edge, sea breeze

galleryItems.alt: Rows of identical white headstones with national flags at the UN Memorial Cemetery
galleryItems.caption: UN Memorial Cemetery — the world's only UN cemetery, 2,300+ graves of soldiers from 21 nations.

galleryItems.alt: Jagalchi Market vendors with displays of fresh fish on ice
galleryItems.caption: Jagalchi Market — Korea's largest seafood market, operated since 1889 on Busan's waterfront.
galleryItems.atmosphere: Live fish stalls, market chatter, second-floor restaurants

galleryItems.alt: Busan Tower rising above Yongdusan Park's pines and mid-rise city skyline
galleryItems.caption: Yongdusan Park & Busan Tower (120 m, built 1973) — the central-Busan viewpoint.

itineraryStops.name: 부산항 픽업 (크루즈 터미널 도착홀)
itineraryStops.description: 대형 크루즈 선박은 부산항 국제크루즈터미널(영도구 동삼동), 중소 선박은 부산항 국제여객터미널(중구)에서 하선합니다. 드라이버와 영어 가이드가 도착홀에서 팀을 맞이합니다.
highlights: Pickup: **Busan Port International Cruise Terminal** (Dongsam) or **International Passenger Terminal** (Choryang) arrival hall
**Small-group van/minicoach (max ~14 pax)** — shared with other cruise passengers
Guide carries 'AtoC Korea — Busan Day Tour' name placard
**Lunch included** at local Busan restaurant
**Boarding 30 min after ship clearance** (immigration buffer)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Single pickup at the cruise terminal keeps small-group logistics simple and reduces no-show risk vs. multi-point pickup. The 09:00 departure lands the group at UN Memorial right after opening (less crowded than 10:30+) and sets up the Taejongdae 10:45 slot before sea-cliff tour-bus peak.

itineraryStops.name: 유엔기념공원 (한국전쟁)
itineraryStops.description: 유엔기념공원(재한유엔기념공원)은 전 세계 유일의 유엔 관리 군인 묘지입니다. 1951년 1월 유엔사령부 설립, 한국전쟁 중 전사한 16개국 2,311명의 유엔군 장병이 장면해 있습니다. 11개 표지석 에비뉴, 현충탑, 기념관을 돌아봅니다. 무료 입장. 방문 시간: 약 60분.
highlights: **ONLY UN memorial cemetery in the world** — established Jan 18, 1951; donated by Korea to UN in 1959
**22 sending nations** honored; **2,300 graves from 11 countries** today
**Wall of Remembrance (2006)** — 40,896 names on 140 marble panels
Main Gate by Korean architect **Kim Joong-up (1966)**
**'Turn Toward Busan' ceremony** Nov 11 at 11:00 AM (synchronized worldwide)
**Free admission**, 100% wheelchair-accessible

**Frank Gaylord US Korean War Monument (2013, dedicated by American Battle Monuments Commission)** — by the same sculptor as the National Mall Korean War Veterans Memorial 'The Column' in Washington DC (1995)
**Adjacent UN Sculpture Park** — 34 sculptures donated by sculptors from 21 nations who fought in the Korean War; opened October 24, 2001 as 50th-anniversary commemoration
**UN General Assembly Resolution 977(X) of December 15, 1955** officially established UNMCK as UN-administered protected territory under international UN Memorial Cemetery Commission
timeUsed: Symbolic Area + 22 sending-state flag plaza (~10 min)
Memorial Service Hall + 12-min English documentary (~15 min)
Wall of Remembrance + Memorabilia Hall (~20 min)
Reflection time + photographs (~10 min)
Walk back to vehicle (~5 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: UNMCK is **the most globally significant Korean War heritage site in Busan** and a sober opener to the day's coastal/cultural circuit. The 09:30 small-group arrival catches it before the 11:00 tour-bus peak — important for a fixed-itinerary group, since cemetery atmosphere degrades rapidly with crowds. Wheelchair-accessibility makes it the lowest-mobility stop of the day; the Wall of Remembrance and 12-min documentary deliver depth without strenuous walking, which suits the small-group's mixed mobility levels. For cruise guests on tight reboarding windows the 60-min dwell is the day's most schedule-flexible stop — visitors can compress to ~30 min while still covering documentary + Symbolic Area + Wall, or extend to 75 min for the Sculpture Park.

sources: American Battle Monuments Commission (US Korean War Monument at Busan)

itineraryStops.name: 태종대 해안 절벽
itineraryStops.description: 태종대(부산기념물 28호)는 영도 최남단 천연 공원으로, 신라 태종무열왕이 이곳에서 활을 쏘았다는 전설에서 이름을 딴 곳입니다. 100m 이상 수직 해안 절벽에서 부산 남해안 최고 전망을 감상합니다. 다누비 열차(₩4,000, 선택)를 타거나 4.3km 절벽 트레일을 걸을 수 있습니다. 망부석 전설 바위, 1906년 건립 등대, 신선대 전망대가 있습니다. 무료 입장(열차 별도). 방문 시간: 약 75분.
highlights: **Busan Monument No. 28** — wave-cut terrace ~120,000 years old (4th interglacial period)
Named after **King Taejong Muyeol of Silla** (604–661 CE, Three Kingdoms unifier)
**Yeongdo Lighthouse — Busan's oldest, built 1906** (10th in Korea); renovated 2004
**3-story Observatory** with views to **Tsushima Island (Japan, 50 km)** on clear days
**Danubi Train** ~₩4,000 RT — 5 stops along 4.3 km coastal loop
**Mangbuseok Rock** legend + **'Light Beyond Limitation' sculpture** (Ji Sul Won Kyung-Lee)
Park free; trails strenuous (lighthouse area = many steep stairs)

**Designated Busan National Geopark site (2013) + State-Designated Cultural Property Scenic Site No. 17 (2005)** — dual heritage status protecting the Cretaceous-era sedimentary cliffs, wave-cut terraces, orbicular hornfels, and slumping structures
**Annual Hydrangea Culture Festival** at Taejongsa Temple (late June – early July) draws 100,000+ visitors to the bigleaf-hydrangea flower carpet inside the park grounds
timeUsed: Coach drop-off + Danubi Train ticket purchase (~10 min)
Train to Observatory + 3-floor panorama (~20 min)
Walk to Yeongdo Lighthouse + 'Light Beyond Limitation' sculpture (~15 min)
Cliff-edge views toward Mangbuseok Rock + Sinseon Rock area (~20 min)
Train back to entrance + reassemble (~10 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Taejongdae is **the canonical Busan natural-scenery anchor** — the day's wild-coast counterpoint to the urban afternoon (Gamcheon → Yongdusan → Jagalchi). The 75-min window with a fixed group focuses on the Danubi Train + Observatory + Lighthouse loop, which the train enables for mixed-mobility small-groups (the strenuous full 4.3 km circuit would split the party). Group buys train tickets together at the entrance to streamline boarding. For cruise guests the 75-min Danubi Train + Observatory + Lighthouse loop is the day's most efficient access to Busan's wild coastline — without the train this stop would require 2+ hours to do justice.

itineraryStops.name: 점심 (부산 현지 식당)
itineraryStops.description: 사하구·감천 인근 부산 현지 식당에서 점심. 주로 돼지국밥(부산식 돼지 수육 국밥) 식당이나 이에 준하는 메뉴. 점심 식사비는 별도(약 ₩10,000~20,000).
highlights: **Lunch INCLUDED** in tour price (₩58.50 USD total)
Typical: **Dwaeji Gukbap** (Busan signature) or Korean set-menu
**Vegetarian / halal with 24h prior notice**
Drinks not included (₩3,000–6,000 if ordered)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Included lunch at a fixed restaurant keeps the small-group on schedule (vs. private-charter where lunch self-pay can stretch 90+ min). The 12:15 slot (vs. private's 12:30) accounts for the slightly faster fixed-restaurant flow.

itineraryStops.name: 감천문화마을
itineraryStops.description: 감천문화마을은 '한국의 마추픽추', '부산의 산토리니'로 불리는 사하구 언덕 위 마을입니다. 약 1만 명이 거주하는 파스텔 색상 계단식 민가로, 2009년 공공미술 프로젝트 이후 부산 대표 문화 명소가 됐습니다. 입구 지도(₩1,000)로 골목 탐방. 어린 왕자 조각상이 인기 포토 스팟. 무료 입장(지도 별도). 방문 시간: 약 75분.
highlights: **1955 Taegeukdo refugee settlement** — ~800 families / ~4,000 people founded by Jo Cheol-je
**Taegeukdo philosophy 'allowing others to prosper'** = no roof blocks neighbor's view
**'Dreaming of Machu Picchu' (2009)** + **'Miro Miro' (2010)** Ministry of Culture art projects
**UN-HABITAT Asian Townscape Award 2012**
**~1.4 million visitors annually**
**Little Prince and Fox bench** at Sky Garden = signature photo (long queue, free)
Stamp Tour map ₩2,000 (9 numbered installations)
Steep alleys + stairs = not stroller-/wheelchair-friendly

**K-content pilgrimage site** — Running Man Episode 126 (Choi Ji-woo guest), Luca: The Beginning (2021), Ode to My Father (2014), A Little Princess; **KTO '100 Must-Visit Tourist Spots in Korea' five consecutive times**
timeUsed: Coach drop-off at upper entrance (~5 min)
Star Viewing Hall + rainbow-roof panorama (~15 min)
Sky Garden + Little Prince & Fox bench photo (~20 min)
Maze-alley walk down through murals + craft shops (~25 min)
Reassemble at lower exit (~10 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Gamcheon is **the visual centerpiece of any Busan day tour** and the small-group's biggest 'wow' photo stop. 75 minutes with a fixed group focuses on the **upper-entrance to Sky Garden to Little Prince bench** flow — the canonical photo path that the guide has done dozens of times — rather than free exploration. Pairs naturally with Yongdusan (next stop) — both hillside vantage points 4 km apart, one residential-artistic, one civic-park. For cruise guests the 75-min Gamcheon dwell is the day's primary photo-grade stop — Sky Garden + Star Viewing Hall + maze-alley descent is more Instagram-shareable than any other Busan stop on this circuit.

sources: Korea Tourism Organization (100 Must-Visit Tourist Spots in Korea designation)

itineraryStops.name: 용두산공원 & 부산타워
itineraryStops.description: 용두산공원(6만9,000㎡)은 남포동 중심부 '용 머리 산'에 위치한 시민 공원으로, 임진왜란 시기 조선 수군의 봉수대가 있던 곳입니다. 부산타워(높이 120m)는 전망대(₩12,000 선택)에서 부산항·영도·남포동 파노라마가 펼쳐집니다. 공원 자체는 무료. 방문 시간: 약 40분.
highlights: **'Dragon Head Mountain'** — historically protective barrier against wokou (Japanese pirates)
Site of **Joseon-era waegwan (Japanese trade enclave 1678–1878)** + **Ryūtōsan Shrine** + Korean War shantytown
**Busan Tower 1973** → rebranded **Busan Diamond Tower 2021** after redevelopment
**Tower top modeled after Bulguksa Dabotap baldaquin** (NT 20, Gyeongju) — Silla heritage quotation
**Statue of Admiral Yi Sun-sin** (1545–1598) — Imjin War turtle-ship hero who never lost a ship
**Citizens' Bell** — 100,000 donors; struck 33× on New Year's Eve + March 1
Tower observatory ₩12,000 adult; park free 24h
**Covered escalator from Gwangbok-ro** — no return escalator

**Citizens' Bell 33-strike ritual** — struck **33 times on New Year's Eve and March 1st (Independence Movement Day)** — the **33 strikes commemorate the 33 Korean signatories of the 1919 Declaration of Independence** read at Tapgol Park in Seoul; ties Busan's civic identity to national independence narrative
timeUsed: Escalator from Gwangbok-ro Fashion Street up to park (~5 min)
Flower clock + Yi Sun-sin statue + Citizens' Bell photos (~10 min)
Tower observatory ascent + 360° panorama (~25 min)
Descent via stairs (~5 min)
Citizens' Bell pavilion + flower clock + bronze dragon photo (~5 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Yongdusan delivers **Busan's most-recognized 360° panorama** — and the tower's quotation of Bulguksa Dabotap connects this stop to the Gyeongju heritage circuit cruise passengers might pair on a multi-port itinerary. **Tower entry is OPTIONAL** for this small-group (₩12,000 self-pay): guests who want the panorama buy tickets onsite while the rest enjoy the park (Yi Sun-sin statue, flower clock, Citizens' Bell). 45-min window covers the escalator up + park + optional tower without forcing. For cruise guests Yongdusan is the day's primary weather-fallback — if rain or fog cancels Taejongdae's outdoor cliff loop, the Diamond Tower observatory + park provides a ~75-min indoor alternative within the same dwell window.

itineraryStops.name: 남포동 & 자갈치수산시장
itineraryStops.description: 자갈치시장은 한국 최대 수산시장으로 부산 남포항 수변에 자리합니다. 갓 잡은 활어 회, 게, 문어 등을 길거리에서 즐기거나 2층 식당을 이용할 수 있습니다. 크루즈 일정상 마지막 이동 전 시장과 남포동 거리를 산책합니다. 무료 입장. 방문 시간: 약 45분.
highlights: **Korea's largest seafood market** — ~400 vendors / 66,000 m²
Name = **'jagal' (gravel)** + **Jagalchi Ajumma** post-war women vendors
**Modern seagull-shaped building reopened 2006** (designed by Hyunjun Mihn)
**Hours 05:00–22:00 daily**; **CLOSED 1st & 3rd Tuesdays**
**Buy-downstairs / eat-upstairs hoe (raw-fish) flow**
Adjacent: **BIFF Square** (Busan Film Festival), **Gukje Market**, **Gwangbok-ro**
October **Jagalchi Cultural Tourism Festival**
Address: **52 Jagalchihaean-ro, Jung-gu**

**Sannakji (live cut octopus) is Busan's culinary signature** — small octopus cut into still-moving pieces, served with sesame oil and salt; the source of *Old Boy* (2003)'s famous octopus scene that introduced the dish to international audiences
timeUsed: Walk from Yongdusan / drop-off near Jagalchi (~5 min)
Ground floor live-tank walkthrough (~15 min)
Second-floor hoe restaurant peek + Nampo-dong street photos (~15 min)
Optional snack purchase + Gwangbok-ro stroll (~10 min)
Stamp + souvenir purchase from Jagalchi Ajumma stalls (~5 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Jagalchi is **the most authentic 'Busan flavor' on a single-day tour** and the small-group's afternoon free-time anchor. 60 minutes (15 more than charter version) accounts for fixed-group reassembly — pairs Jagalchi's live-tank ground floor with Nampo-dong / BIFF Square / Gukje Market street walking. Optional fish cake (eomuk) snack stop that small-group budgets handle better than charter's premium positioning. For cruise guests this is the most authentic 'Busan flavor' photo opportunity in 45 minutes — live tanks + ajumma vendor culture + seagull-roof architecture in a walk-through that ends close to the ship's reboarding zone.

itineraryStops.name: 부산항 귀환
itineraryStops.description: 출항 최소 60분 전 크루즈 터미널 귀환. 선박 출항 일정을 실시간으로 파악하며 운행합니다.
highlights: Direct group return to original cruise terminal
**60–90 min buffer before all-aboard**
**No individual hotel drop-offs** (small-group fixed schedule)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Direct return without intermediate stops protects the all-aboard buffer for all passengers simultaneously. 30 minutes covers worst-case rush-hour transit from Nampo-dong back to either terminal.

routePhases.description: 크루즈 터미널에서 남구 유엔기념공원까지 이동(약 30분) + 하선 소요 시간.
유엔공원(60분) 후 약 30분 이동, 태종대 해안 절벽 탐방 75분.
사하구·감천 인근 부산 현지 식당 점심.
감천문화마을 포토 루프(75분), 이후 용두산공원 부산타워 전망.
남포동·자갈치시장 길거리 음식 및 시장 탐방 후 크루즈 터미널 귀환.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A circular shore-excursion route built around Busan's port. Unlike the Incheon equivalent, Busan's main attractions are within 30 minutes of the cruise terminal — meaning more time at sights and less time on the road. The route hits the four most-photographed Busan stops (Gamcheon, Taejongdae, Jagalchi, Busan Tower) plus the city's most thoughtful Korean War heritage site (UN Cemetery), all coordinated to your ship's all-aboard time. The 4.9/5 rating across 32 reviews reflects how tightly this circuit is calibrated.

bestFor: Cruise passengers with a 9+ hour port call in Busan
First-time visitors who want a single-day Busan overview
Travelers prioritizing coast + culture + market in one route
lessIdealFor: Cruisers with a port call shorter than ≈9 hours

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: All five Busan signatures, with a return buffer
items.detail: Many cruise day tours skip either the UN Cemetery (too sober for a beach day) or Gamcheon (too far from the port). This route includes both, plus Taejongdae, Yongdusan/Busan Tower, and Jagalchi/Nampo-dong, and still leaves a 60-minute safety buffer for the return drive. The 9-hour window is built around realistic Busan traffic, not a brochure-ideal schedule.

items.label: Built for the cruise schedule, not the operator's clock
items.detail: Pickup is calibrated to your ship's actual arrival, not a fixed start. Ships that arrive before 07:00 trigger an early-bird option (₩10,000/person surcharge) so you don't waste time at the terminal. Drop-off is timed to leave at least a 60-minute buffer before your stated all-aboard time.

items.label: Certified bilingual guide, no shopping detours
items.detail: Guides are licensed by the Korea Tourism Organization and fluent in English and Chinese. There are no ginseng shops, duty-free stops, or commissioned souvenir detours — every minute of the day is at attractions or in transit. This is rarer than it sounds for cruise group tours.

items.label: Shared-van price between coach and private tour
items.detail: Private Busan cruise excursions run $300–500+ per group. Coach tours run 40+ passengers at a low per-person price. This shared-van version sits between, with up to ≈10 passengers per departure — accessible for solo cruisers, couples, and small groups who don't need a private vehicle. Trade-off: fixed circuit, not customizable.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Air-conditioned shared van, certified English/Chinese-speaking guide, fuel/tolls/parking.
content: Air-conditioned shared van, certified English/Chinese-speaking guide, fuel/tolls/parking.
Not included: lunch (≈₩10,000–₩20,000), optional Taejongdae Danubi Train (₩4,000 adult), optional Busan Tower observatory (₩12,000 adult), Gamcheon stamp-tour map (₩2,000), tips.
UN Cemetery, Yongdusan Park, Gamcheon village, and Jagalchi Market are all free to enter.
Total typical out-of-pocket: ₩20,000–₩40,000 per person.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Group meets at the Busan Port International Passenger Terminal arrival hall.
content: Group meets at the Busan Port International Passenger Terminal arrival hall.
Pickup time is calibrated to your ship's actual disembarkation, not a fixed start.
Provide ship name, port-call date, expected disembarkation time, and all-aboard time at booking.
Early pickup (before 08:00) adds ₩10,000 per person.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Sail-away timing
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Drop-off at the cruise terminal at least 60 minutes before your all-aboard time.
content: Drop-off at the cruise terminal at least 60 minutes before your all-aboard time.
Cruise rule of thumb: be back on the ship at least 30 minutes before stated departure.
The guide tracks every passenger's individual ship schedule and prioritizes return order if multiple ships sail at different times.
The route is built with a 60-minute buffer for return-drive traffic.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
content: Moderate.
Gamcheon Village has steep alleys and stairs (built into a hillside) — not wheelchair-friendly, and stroller-difficult.
Taejongdae's full cliff loop is 4.3 km but the optional Danubi Train (₩4,000) avoids the climb.
UN Cemetery and Yongdusan are flat.
Total walking: ≈3–5 km depending on Taejongdae/Gamcheon choices.
Comfortable closed-toe shoes essential.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before pickup.
content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before pickup.
Same-day cancellations and no-shows non-refundable.
If your ship's port call is cancelled by the cruise line, full refund with cancellation documentation.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Children age 8 and up are welcome and the route works well for them — Taejongdae's Danubi…
content: Children age 8 and up are welcome and the route works well for them — Taejongdae's Danubi Train is a kid favorite, and Jagalchi's live fish are reliably interesting.
Strollers are not viable at Gamcheon (stairs); other stops accept them.
Wheelchair users should consider a private cruise tour with route adjustments — Gamcheon and Taejongdae's cliff trail are the bottlenecks.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Busan is South Korea's southernmost major city — milder and wetter than Seoul, with mild winters (5–10 °C lows). Summer is humid, with peak rainfall in July monsoon.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms in late March / early April.
Hot and humid; July monsoon possible. Sea breeze keeps Taejongdae mild.
Best season — clearest air, warmest sea. Foliage in late October.
Mild for Korea but can be windy at coastal stops.

seasonalVariations.description: Cherry blossoms at Yongdusan Park and along the Taejongdae coastal road in late March / early April. Best season for cruise calls.
Long daylight; coastal Busan stays cooler than inland Korea thanks to sea breeze. July monsoon rain possible — check forecast.
Clearest visibility for Tsushima views from Taejongdae; foliage in late October. Best season overall.
Fewer crowds; clear sea views; mild compared to Seoul (Busan winter lows 5–10 °C).

bookingTrustItems.title: 공인 현지 운영사
bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance
bookingTrustItems.body: 귀선 시각 최소 60분 전 부산항 귀환 보장 — 크루즈 물류 전문 운영사.

bookingTrustItems.title: 코스 전문
bookingTrustItems.description: Hand-tuned itinerary, not a generic loop
bookingTrustItems.body: 부산 크루즈 기항지 투어를 매일 운행하는 코스 전문가 — 크루즈 도착 시각에 맞춘 코스 설계.

bookingTrustItems.title: 그룹 규모 관리
bookingTrustItems.body: 최대 10인 합승 밴으로 감천의 좁은 골목길을 여유롭게 이동합니다.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 즉시 확인
bookingSupportSteps.description: 예약 확인서 발송. 선박명, 크루즈 터미널(동삼부두 또는 국제여객터미널)을 알려주시면 픽업 지점을 확정합니다.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 12시간 전 알림
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Weather update and any route adjustments
bookingSupportSteps.description: 픽업 장소 및 집결 시각 안내.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 최종 픽업 안내
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Exact pickup time and driver contact

bookingSupportSteps.title: 당일 코스 안내
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Morning briefing based on live conditions
bookingSupportSteps.description: 가이드가 크루즈 터미널 도착홀에서 직접 맞이합니다.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 경유지별 심층 안내
bookingSupportSteps.description: 귀선 일정에 충분한 여유를 두고 크루즈 터미널 하차 — 안전한 귀선 보장.

bookingSupportSteps.title: 투어 후 지원
bookingSupportSteps.description: 귀환 후 간단한 피드백 요청 전송.

staticQuestions.question: 크루즈 성수기에 투어가 다 찰 수 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 합승 밴은 최대 약 10인입니다. 크루즈 성수기에는 운영사가 여러 밴을 동시에 운행하며, 한 팀의 인원이 많아지더라도 그룹을 분리하여 진행합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 선박이 매우 일찍 도착하면 합류할 수 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 네, 조기 픽업(08:00 이전)은 1인당 ₩10,000 추가 비용으로 가능합니다. 06:00 도착 크루즈에 유용합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 점심이 포함되어 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 포함되지 않습니다. 가이드가 현지 식당을 안내해 드립니다. 예산: 약 ₩10,000~20,000.

staticQuestions.question: 프라이빗 부산 크루즈 투어와 어떻게 다른가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 프라이빗 투어는 그룹 전용으로 코스 맞춤이 가능하며 가격이 더 높습니다(보통 1인 $90~120+). 이 소그룹 투어는 다른 크루즈 여행자와 합승하여 가격이 낮습니다.

staticQuestions.question: 감천 계단이 힘들면 어떻게 하나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 감천 주요 포토존은 버스 하차 지점에서 짧게 걸어서 닿을 수 있습니다. 깊은 탐방에만 가파른 계단이 필요합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 일정을 맞춤 변경할 수 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 이 투어는 고정 코스 공유 상품입니다. 가이드가 각 경유지 체류 시간을 소폭 조정할 수 있지만(예: 감천에서 더 오래 머물기), 경유지 교체는 불가합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 부산타워 전망대 입장권이 포함되나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 포함되지 않습니다. 전망대는 선택 사항(성인 ₩12,000). 용두산공원 자체는 무료이며 도시 전망을 즐길 수 있습니다.

staticQuestions.question: 태종대에서 다누비 열차를 타야 하나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 절벽 트레일(4.3km)이 걱정되신다면 다누비 열차(성인 ₩4,000)를 추천합니다. 약 30분에 트레일을 커버합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 점심 대신 자갈치에서 식사할 수 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 투어 일정상 자갈치는 오후 4시 이후 방문이라 정식 점심은 어렵습니다. 길거리 음식과 간식은 즐길 수 있습니다.

staticQuestions.question: 부산을 처음 방문하는 분에게 적합한가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 네, 기념관·해안·예술 마을·전망대·수산시장으로 이어지는 부산 핵심을 균형 있게 돌아보는 투어입니다.

staticQuestions.question: 도보 이동이 얼마나 되나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 선택에 따라 약 3~5km. 감천 언덕 골목과 태종대 절벽 트레일이 활동적인 구간. 유엔공원과 남포동은 평탄합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 어린이와 함께하기 적합한가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 만 8세 이상, 감천 계단을 소화할 수 있는 어린이에게 적합합니다. 태종대 다누비 열차는 어린이들이 좋아합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 어르신도 참여할 수 있나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 활동적인 어르신에게 적합합니다. 다누비 열차로 태종대를 해결하고, 감천 주요 포토존은 깊이 들어가지 않아도 됩니다.

staticQuestions.question: 권장 최소 연령은 몇 세인가요?
staticQuestions.answer: 만 8세 이상, 감천 계단과 하루 일정을 소화할 수 있는 어린이에게 적합합니다.

staticQuestions.question: 폭우나 태풍이 오면 어떻게 되나요?
staticQuestions.answer: 가벼운 비: 예정대로 진행. 강우 시: 태종대 절벽 트레일 통제 가능(다누비 열차는 계속 운행). 감천 위쪽 구간은 미끄럼 주의. 크루즈 기항 자체가 취소되는 태풍 급 기상은 선사에 문의하세요.

props.headlineLine1: Cruise port to painted alleys, market to memorial —
props.headlineLine2: Busan's headline circuit in a single shared-van day

hero.tagline: Five Busan signatures coordinated with your ship's schedule, on a shared van with other cruisers

meta.duration: ≈ 9 hours, calibrated to your cruise's port-call window

galleryItems.alt: Colorful houses cascading down a hillside in Gamcheon Culture Village
galleryItems.caption: Gamcheon Culture Village — painted in 2009 as part of Busan's public-art renewal of the postwar refugee neighborhood.
galleryItems.atmosphere: Bright pastels, narrow alleys, hillside stairs

galleryItems.alt: Sea cliffs with a white lighthouse on the horizon
galleryItems.caption: Taejongdae coastal park, southern tip of Yeongdo Island. Tsushima visible on clear winter days.
galleryItems.atmosphere: Open ocean, cliff edge, sea breeze

galleryItems.alt: Rows of identical white headstones with national flags at the UN Memorial Cemetery
galleryItems.caption: UN Memorial Cemetery — the world's only UN cemetery, 2,300+ graves of soldiers from 21 nations.

galleryItems.alt: Jagalchi Market vendors with displays of fresh fish on ice
galleryItems.caption: Jagalchi Market — Korea's largest seafood market, operated since 1889 on Busan's waterfront.
galleryItems.atmosphere: Live fish stalls, market chatter, second-floor restaurants

galleryItems.alt: Busan Tower rising above Yongdusan Park's pines and mid-rise city skyline
galleryItems.caption: Yongdusan Park & Busan Tower (120 m, built 1973) — the central-Busan viewpoint.

itineraryStops.name: Busan Port pickup (cruise terminal arrival hall)
itineraryStops.description: Group meets the guide in the Busan Port International Passenger Terminal arrival hall. Pickup time is calibrated to your specific ship's actual disembarkation, not a fixed 08:30 — the 08:30 here is a typical example. Provide ship name, port-call date, expected arrival time, and all-aboard time at booking; the guide tracks the schedule and adjusts pickup automatically. Ships that arrive before 07:00 trigger an early-pickup option (₩10,000/person surcharge to the guide for the early start) so you don't waste two hours at the terminal.
timeUsed: Operational logistics (pickup/transfer)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Required logistics; calibrated to the specific cruise.

itineraryStops.name: UN Memorial Cemetery (Korean War)
itineraryStops.description: The world's only United Nations cemetery, established in 1951 during the Korean War and granted permanent UN status by the General Assembly in 1955. Holds 2,300+ graves of soldiers from 21 nations who fought under the UN Command, with the largest national groups from the United Kingdom (885), Türkiye (462), Canada (378), and Australia (281). The grounds include a Memorial Hall with multilingual exhibits, a Wall of Remembrance listing all 40,896 UN Command war dead, and the daily flag ceremony at 10:00 (subject to weather). The cemetery is in Nam-gu, ≈30 minutes east of Yeongdo by road. Free entry; open daily 09:00–17:00 except Korean New Year and Chuseok.
timeUsed: Arrival, ticketing, and orientation briefing
Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Essential context for the Korean War's UN dimension; quiet, flat, fits early-morning energy.

itineraryStops.name: Taejongdae coastal cliffs
itineraryStops.description: Coastal park on the southern tip of Yeongdo Island, named after King Taejong Muyeol of Silla (r. 654–661 CE), the king who allied with Tang China to unify the Korean peninsula. The 4.3 km loop trail circles cliffs that drop ≈100 m to the East Sea, passing the Yeongdo Lighthouse (1906) and an observatory point where, on clear winter days, Tsushima Island in Japan is visible ≈50 km offshore. Most cruise visitors use the Danubi Train (₩4,000 adult, ₩3,000 youth, optional, ≈30 min loop) instead of walking the full circuit. Free park entry; the lighthouse area is wheelchair-friendly but the cliff trail is not.
timeUsed: Arrival, ticketing, and orientation briefing
Main attraction exploration with photo time
Wrap-up and reassembly at vehicle
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Open-sea cliff scenery is Busan's signature landscape — distinct from the rest of the day's urban stops.

itineraryStops.name: Lunch (local Busan restaurant)
itineraryStops.description: Sit-down restaurant chosen for English/Chinese menus and proximity to the next stop. Typical local choices: dwaeji-gukbap (Busan-style pork-and-rice soup, the city's signature, ≈₩10,000), milmyeon (cold wheat noodles, ≈₩9,000), or a bibimbap set. Lunch is paid by the guest; budget ₩10,000–₩20,000 per person. Vegetarian, halal, and gluten-free options possible with advance notice at booking.
timeUsed: Operational logistics (meal break)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Mid-day refuel between coastal and inland stops.

itineraryStops.name: Gamcheon Culture Village
itineraryStops.description: Hillside village originally built in the 1950s by Korean War refugees, now Busan's most-photographed neighborhood. The current colorful look dates to 2009, when the city sponsored the 'Dreaming of Machu Picchu in Busan' public-art project — local artists painted the houses, installed murals and small sculptures (the Little Prince & Fox bench is the most-photographed), and added the staircase mosaics. Houses are still occupied; the village asks visitors to keep voices down in residential lanes. The main photo route is a 30–40 minute walk on steep alleys and stairs — uphill at the start, downhill back. Most main viewpoints are reachable from the bus drop-off; deeper exploration requires more stairs. Optional 'stamp tour' map ₩2,000.
timeUsed: Arrival, ticketing, and orientation briefing
Main attraction exploration with photo time
Wrap-up and reassembly at vehicle
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Visual centerpiece of the day. Most cruisers come specifically for Gamcheon.

itineraryStops.name: Yongdusan Park & Busan Tower
itineraryStops.description: Yongdusan ('Dragon Head Mountain') sits in central Busan above Nampo-dong; the 120-meter Diamond Tower (built 1973, renamed Busan Tower; observatory restored to public access in 2021 after a 2017 closure) tops out at 159 m above sea level and gives a panoramic view of the port, Yeongdo, Gwangan Bridge, and on clear days back to Taejongdae. Park entry is free and includes the statue of Admiral Yi Sun-sin and the Bell of Citizens; observatory ticket is ₩12,000 adult and is the only paid element. Reaching the park from Nampo Station: 8-minute walk uphill, or a covered outdoor escalator from the ground level.
timeUsed: Arrival, ticketing, and orientation briefing
Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Compact orientation stop after Gamcheon. Walking distance to the next stop.

itineraryStops.name: Nampo-dong & Jagalchi Fish Market
itineraryStops.description: Korea's largest seafood market, operating on this Yeongdo-side waterfront since 1889 (formalized after 1945 when the Japanese-era market was reorganized by displaced Korean female fishmongers — Jagalchi means 'pebble-place' for the original beach). Modern indoor building has two floors: ground floor stalls sell live fish, shellfish, and dried seafood; second floor has restaurants where you can take a fish bought downstairs and have it prepared (sliced raw as hwe, grilled, or stewed) for a cooking fee of ₩10,000–₩20,000. Stalls are run mostly by women across multiple generations of the same families. Open ≈05:00–22:00 daily; closed first and third Tuesdays of the month. Nampo-dong's BIFF Square (Korean film stars' handprints, like Hollywood's Walk of Fame) and Gukje Market are within 5 minutes' walk.
timeUsed: Arrival, ticketing, and orientation briefing
Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 5-minute drive (or 10-minute walk) from Yongdusan; closes the day with a sensory peak.

itineraryStops.name: Return to Busan Port
itineraryStops.description: Jagalchi/Nampo to the Busan Port International Passenger Terminal is ≈5 km, 15–25 minutes by road. Drop-off is at the terminal entrance with a minimum 60-minute buffer before your ship's all-aboard time. The guide tracks each passenger's individual ship schedule and prioritizes return order if multiple ships sail at different times.
timeUsed: Operational logistics (return)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Required logistics; on-time return is the operator's stated commitment.

routePhases.description: Cruise terminal to UN Memorial Cemetery in Nam-gu (≈30 min) plus disembarkation buffer.
UN Cemetery (60 min) then drive 30 min to Taejongdae for 75 min of coastal cliffs.
Local Busan restaurant near Saha-gu / Gamcheon transition.
Gamcheon Culture Village photo loop (75 min), then Yongdusan Park's central viewpoint (45 min).
Walk from Yongdusan down to Nampo-dong / Jagalchi Market for streetfood and seafood.
Drop-off at cruise terminal at least 60 min before all-aboard.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A circular shore-excursion route built around Busan's port. Unlike the Incheon equivalent, Busan's main attractions are within 30 minutes of the cruise terminal — meaning more time at sights and less time on the road. The route hits the four most-photographed Busan stops (Gamcheon, Taejongdae, Jagalchi, Busan Tower) plus the city's most thoughtful Korean War heritage site (UN Cemetery), all coordinated to your ship's all-aboard time. The 4.9/5 rating across 32 reviews reflects how tightly this circuit is calibrated.

bestFor: Cruise passengers with a 9+ hour port call in Busan
First-time visitors who want a single-day Busan overview
Travelers prioritizing coast + culture + market in one route
lessIdealFor: Cruisers with a port call shorter than ≈9 hours

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: All five Busan signatures, with a return buffer
items.detail: Many cruise day tours skip either the UN Cemetery (too sober for a beach day) or Gamcheon (too far from the port). This route includes both, plus Taejongdae, Yongdusan/Busan Tower, and Jagalchi/Nampo-dong, and still leaves a 60-minute safety buffer for the return drive. The 9-hour window is built around realistic Busan traffic, not a brochure-ideal schedule.

items.label: Built for the cruise schedule, not the operator's clock
items.detail: Pickup is calibrated to your ship's actual arrival, not a fixed start. Ships that arrive before 07:00 trigger an early-bird option (₩10,000/person surcharge) so you don't waste time at the terminal. Drop-off is timed to leave at least a 60-minute buffer before your stated all-aboard time.

items.label: Certified bilingual guide, no shopping detours
items.detail: Guides are licensed by the Korea Tourism Organization and fluent in English and Chinese. There are no ginseng shops, duty-free stops, or commissioned souvenir detours — every minute of the day is at attractions or in transit. This is rarer than it sounds for cruise group tours.

items.label: Shared-van price between coach and private tour
items.detail: Private Busan cruise excursions run $300–500+ per group. Coach tours run 40+ passengers at a low per-person price. This shared-van version sits between, with up to ≈10 passengers per departure — accessible for solo cruisers, couples, and small groups who don't need a private vehicle. Trade-off: fixed circuit, not customizable.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.content: Air-conditioned shared van, certified English/Chinese-speaking guide, fuel/tolls/parking. Not included: lunch (≈₩10,000–₩20,000), optional Taejongdae Danubi Train (₩4,000 adult), optional Busan Tower observatory (₩12,000 adult), Gamcheon stamp-tour map (₩2,000), tips. UN Cemetery, Yongdusan Park, Gamcheon village, and Jagalchi Market are all free to enter. Total typical out-of-pocket: ₩20,000–₩40,000 per person.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.content: Group meets at the Busan Port International Passenger Terminal arrival hall. Pickup time is calibrated to your ship's actual disembarkation, not a fixed start. Provide ship name, port-call date, expected disembarkation time, and all-aboard time at booking. Early pickup (before 08:00) adds ₩10,000 per person.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Sail-away timing
practicalAccordionItems.content: Drop-off at the cruise terminal at least 60 minutes before your all-aboard time. Cruise rule of thumb: be back on the ship at least 30 minutes before stated departure. The guide tracks every passenger's individual ship schedule and prioritizes return order if multiple ships sail at different times. The route is built with a 60-minute buffer for return-drive traffic.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
practicalAccordionItems.content: Moderate. Gamcheon Village has steep alleys and stairs (built into a hillside) — not wheelchair-friendly, and stroller-difficult. Taejongdae's full cliff loop is 4.3 km but the optional Danubi Train (₩4,000) avoids the climb. UN Cemetery and Yongdusan are flat. Total walking: ≈3–5 km depending on Taejongdae/Gamcheon choices. Comfortable closed-toe shoes essential.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before pickup. Same-day cancellations and no-shows non-refundable. If your ship's port call is cancelled by the cruise line, full refund with cancellation documentation.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Children & accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.content: Children age 8 and up are welcome and the route works well for them — Taejongdae's Danubi Train is a kid favorite, and Jagalchi's live fish are reliably interesting. Strollers are not viable at Gamcheon (stairs); other stops accept them. Wheelchair users should consider a private cruise tour with route adjustments — Gamcheon and Taejongdae's cliff trail are the bottlenecks.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Busan is South Korea's southernmost major city — milder and wetter than Seoul, with mild winters (5–10 °C lows). Summer is humid, with peak rainfall in July monsoon.

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms in late March / early April.
Hot and humid; July monsoon possible. Sea breeze keeps Taejongdae mild.
Best season — clearest air, warmest sea. Foliage in late October.
Mild for Korea but can be windy at coastal stops.

seasonalVariations.highlight: Cherry blossoms at Yongdusan Park and along the Taejongdae coastal road in late March / early April. Best season for cruise calls.
Long daylight; coastal Busan stays cooler than inland Korea thanks to sea breeze. July monsoon rain possible — check forecast.
Clearest visibility for Tsushima views from Taejongdae; foliage in late October. Best season overall.
Fewer crowds; clear sea views; mild compared to Seoul (Busan winter lows 5–10 °C).

bookingTrustItems.title: Licensed local operator
bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance

bookingTrustItems.description: Hand-tuned itinerary, not a generic loop

bookingSupportSteps.detail: Weather update and any route adjustments

staticQuestions.question: Will the tour be full on busy cruise days?
staticQuestions.answer: The shared van seats up to ≈10 passengers. On peak cruise days the operator runs multiple vans simultaneously rather than overcrowding a single one — group dynamics are part of the format and you'll usually be with cruisers from several different ships.

staticQuestions.question: What if my ship arrives very early — can I still join?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. Early pickup (before 08:00) is available with a ₩10,000 per-person surcharge. This is useful for cruises with 06:00–07:00 arrivals where the standard 08:30 start would otherwise leave you waiting two hours at the terminal.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: No. Lunch is free time at a guide-recommended local Busan restaurant; budget ₩10,000–₩20,000 per person. Common choices are dwaeji-gukbap (Busan-style pork-and-rice soup), milmyeon (cold wheat noodles), or a bibimbap set. The guide can route to vegetarian, halal, or gluten-free kitchens with advance notice at booking.

staticQuestions.question: How is this different from a private Busan cruise tour?
staticQuestions.answer: Private cruise tours run only for your group, with a customizable itinerary and door-of-ship pickup, typically $90–$120+ per person (or $300–$500+ per group). This shared-van version runs at $58.50 per person on a fixed circuit. Choose this for budget; choose private if you want flexibility, accessibility, or to skip standard stops.

staticQuestions.question: What if Gamcheon's stairs are too much?
staticQuestions.answer: Gamcheon's main photo zones are reachable by short walk from the bus drop-off — only deeper exploration requires the steep stairs. The guide can route you through the accessible upper section of the village. If mobility is a concern, advise at booking; for full accessibility a private tour with route adjustments is a better fit.

staticQuestions.question: Can I customize the itinerary?
staticQuestions.answer: Limited — this is a fixed-route shared product. The guide can adjust timing within the route (spend longer at Gamcheon, less at Yongdusan, etc.) but cannot swap or drop stops. For full customization, a private Busan cruise tour is the right choice.

staticQuestions.question: Is the Busan Tower observatory ticket included?
staticQuestions.answer: No — the observatory is optional (₩12,000 adult, ₩9,000 youth). Yongdusan Park itself is free and gives a strong city view at ground level. Most groups skip the observatory due to the time cost vs. the time available; if you want to go up, advise the guide on arrival so they can build it into your stop time.

staticQuestions.question: Should we take the Danubi Train at Taejongdae?
staticQuestions.answer: If you have any concern about the 4.3 km cliff loop, yes — the Danubi Train (₩4,000 adult) covers the loop in ≈30 minutes with stops at the lighthouse and the cliff viewpoint, and it solves the climb at the start. Active walkers can do the trail in 60–75 minutes. The 75-minute stop time accommodates either choice.

staticQuestions.question: Can we eat at Jagalchi instead of the lunch restaurant?
staticQuestions.answer: The route reaches Jagalchi at 16:00 — past lunch and before dinner — so eating there means streetfood snacks, hwe (raw sliced fish from a stall), or just browsing. If your priority is a Jagalchi seafood meal, advise at booking; the guide can move the lunch slot but the tour's pacing assumes the standard mid-day restaurant break.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to Busan?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — it covers the most distinctive Busan stops in a single balanced day, with a mix of memorial, coast, art village, viewpoint, and market. First-time visitors with one cruise port-call day are the primary audience for this product.

staticQuestions.question: How much walking is there?
staticQuestions.answer: ≈3–5 km depending on choices. Gamcheon's hillside alleys and Taejongdae's cliff trail are the active sections; UN Cemetery and Yongdusan are flat. The Danubi Train at Taejongdae is an option to skip the climb. Comfortable closed-toe shoes are essential.

staticQuestions.question: Is this suitable for families with children?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for school-age children (≈8+) who can manage Gamcheon's stairs. Kids tend to respond best to the Danubi Train at Taejongdae and the live-fish floor at Jagalchi. The UN Cemetery is short and quiet.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Active seniors are fine; the Danubi Train solves Taejongdae and the Gamcheon upper section is mostly walkable. Travelers with significant mobility limitations should consider a private cruise tour instead — Gamcheon's hillside is the bottleneck.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 8 and up. Children younger than that may struggle with Gamcheon's stairs and the full-day pace. If you bring younger kids, advise at booking so the guide can plan around the limit.

staticQuestions.question: What if there's heavy rain or a typhoon?
staticQuestions.answer: Light rain: tour operates as planned. Heavy rain: Taejongdae's cliff trail closes; the Danubi Train still runs. Gamcheon stays open but is photo-limited. If a typhoon warning is issued, the operator offers rebooking or a full refund. If the cruise line cancels your port call, full refund with documentation.

v17_schema_unification.description: Phase 1: Converted matching_profile scores from 1-5 int scale to 0-1 float scale to match the 3 Step 3 new tours. Mapping 1→0.20, 2→0.40, 3→0.60, 4→0.80, 5→1.00. Mechanical conversion preserves ordinal information; verified-fact recalibration happens in subsequent enhancement batches.
